proof: v0.13 RRTMG g-point chunking — numerical inertness + peak-VRAM reduction

what_changed.sw: Two-stream (reftra/vrtqdr) + g-point reduction tiled over the 14-band axis with a SEQUENTIAL lax.scan (_sw_band_scan_fluxes): the band axis is carved by lax.dynamic_slice (no transpose copy) and each tile's (band,g-point)-summed flux is accumulated in fp64 in the scan carry. Knob: _SW_GPOINT_CHUNK_BANDS (default 1, snapped to a divisor of 14 by _sw_chunk_bands). The scan's carry dependency forces XLA to free each tile's two-stream working set before the next.
what_changed.lw: rtrnmc band loop production path (_lw_solver_fluxes / flux_only) accumulates fp64 band-summed fluxes one band-tile at a time instead of stacking all 16 bands into a (ncol,nlay+1,16,16) array. Knob: _LW_GPOINT_CHUNK_BANDS (default 1). Oracle entry (compute_rrtmg_lw_intermediates) still returns the full per-g-point arrays (WRF parity preserved).
what_changed.api: Public API + defaults of solve_rrtmg_sw_column / solve_rrtmg_lw_column unchanged. Tile-size knobs added at module scope.

inertness.method: CPU fp64. The 'unchunked' reference is the chunk = n_bands configuration of the SAME tiled code path (one tile == original single-pass); each candidate chunk width is bit-compared against it on the analytic SW/LW column fixtures (3 columns x 16 layers). Reproduce: PYTHONPATH=src JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu python proofs/v013/gpoint_chunk_rrtmg.py --mode inertness

vs_legacy_float32_band_sum.note: The original SW code reduced the (band, g-point) axes in the working float32 two-stream precision; the chunked path reduces in fp64. This is a one-time, knob-independent precision IMPROVEMENT of <=~5e-5 rel on the SW fixture (flux_down 2.5458984 -> 2.5458433, marginally closer to the WRF oracle). LW already worked in fp64, so LW is bit-identical (0.0) to the legacy full-array sum.

energy_closure_invariant_chunked_sw.note: fp64 machine-epsilon: heating rate / flux divergence / column absorption stay mutually consistent.

vram.method: GPU RTX 5090 32 GiB. XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_PREALLOCATE=false (BFC on-demand). The kernel is wrapped in jax.jit because the operational path runs the radiation column kernel inside a jitted lax.scan — only under jit does XLA do whole-program memory planning. device.memory_stats()['peak_bytes_in_use']. Columns built by tiling the 3-column fixture; vertical levels deepened by repeating the layer axis. One fresh process per config. Reproduce: XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_PREALLOCATE=false PYTHONPATH=src python proofs/v013/gpoint_chunk_rrtmg.py --mode vram --kind sw --chunk 1 --ncol 24576 --nrep 3 (via the jit-wrapped run_vram).

sw_intermediate_chunk_caveat.note: chunk=1 (one band per scan tile) is the default and the most robust/lowest peak. INTERMEDIATE tile widths can be WORSE: at nlev=48 ncol=24576, chunk=2 = 11051 MiB (34% reduction) but chunk=7 OOM'd (24.3 GiB attempt) because XLA schedules the two large tiles with overlapping live buffers. The default is therefore the smallest tile.

deep_column_unchunked_oom.note: The unchunked SW two-stream working set alone demanded 84.23 GiB (> 32 GiB GPU). chunk=1 streams it band-by-band; the family of allocation that drove the 24h-nested-1km+GWD OOM.

vram.lw_note: LW is VRAM-NEUTRAL. chunk=16 and chunk=1 give the identical 12496 MiB peak at nlev=16, and BOTH OOM at the IDENTICAL upstream allocation (23.34 GiB at nlev=32 ncol=49152; 34.59 GiB at nlev=48 ncol=49152) — proving the LW peak floor is the upstream _lw_solver_base arrays (taumol branch+fallback tau/fracs + planck tables, each ~(ncol,nlay,16,16) fp64), NOT the flux stack. The LW flux-stack accumulation is numerically inert + removes the explicit 16-way stack from the HLO (defensive) but does not lower peak. Cutting the LW peak requires chunking taumol (out of scope for this g-point-flux sprint).

verdict.numerically_inert: YES — result bit-identical (max_rel 0.0) across ALL chunk widths for both SW and LW; energy closure to 3.6e-15; LW oracle intermediates unchanged; full RRTMG test suite shows ZERO new failures vs base 6d051f8.
verdict.sw_vram_reduced: YES — 45-57% peak reduction under jit via the sequential lax.scan (chunk=1), growing with column depth; addresses the deep-column OOM (unchunked two-stream alone is 84 GiB at nlev=80).
verdict.lw_vram_reduced: NEUTRAL — numerically inert; peak unchanged because the LW floor is the upstream taumol/fracs/planck arrays, not the flux stack.
